source.title: Crypto exchange Coinbase to cut about 14% of workforce — Reuters (May 5, 2026)
source.excerpt: Coinbase said on Tuesday it will cut about 700 jobs, or about 14% of its global workforce, as part of a restructuring plan aimed at reducing costs and repositioning the business for the AI era. Coinbase expects to incur about $50 million to $60 million in total restructuring expenses, mainly tied to employee severance and other termination benefits.

source.title: Coinbase Cuts 14% Of Workforce, Signals AI-Driven Future — Bitcoin Magazine
source.excerpt: Coinbase had 4,951 employees as of December 31, 2025, placing the number of affected workers at an estimated 693 people. Departing U.S. employees will receive a minimum of 16 weeks of base pay, plus two weeks per year of service, their next equity vest, and six months of COBRA health coverage.

source.title: Coinbase Reduces Staff by 14% — Markets Media (full CEO memo reproduced)
source.excerpt: Today I've made the difficult decision to reduce the size of Coinbase by ~14%. Two forces are converging at the same time. We need to be front footed to respond to both. First, the market... Second, AI is changing how we work. Over the past year, I've watched engineers use AI to ship in days what used to take a team weeks.

payload.value: Two forces are converging at the same time. First, the market — we're currently in a down market and need to adjust our cost structure. Second, AI is changing how we work. Over the past year, I've watched engineers use AI to ship in days what used to take a team weeks. (Brian Armstrong, CEO memo, May 5 2026)

First, the market... Second, AI is changing how we work. Over the past year, I've watched engineers use AI to ship in days what used to take a team weeks.

source.title: Coinbase Reduces Staff by 14% — Markets Media (full CEO memo reproduced)
source.excerpt: Today I've made the difficult decision to reduce the size of Coinbase by ~14%. Two forces are converging at the same time. We need to be front footed to respond to both. First, the market... Second, AI is changing how we work. Over the past year, I've watched engineers use AI to ship in days what used to take a team weeks.

payload.value: Cross-org; org structure flattening to max 5 layers below CEO/COO; elimination of pure management roles; AI-native pods replacing traditional teams; experiments with one-person teams (engineering + design + product in single role).

source.title: Coinbase Cuts 14% of Staff Amid Crypto 'Down Market,' AI Adoption: CEO — Decrypt
source.excerpt: We are not just reducing headcount and cutting costs — we're fundamentally changing how we operate: rebuilding Coinbase as an intelligence, with humans around the edge aligning it. The restructuring will flatten Coinbase's org chart to five layers maximum below the CEO and COO.

source.title: Coinbase Reduces Staff by 14% — Markets Media (full CEO memo reproduced)
source.excerpt: Today I've made the difficult decision to reduce the size of Coinbase by ~14%. Two forces are converging at the same time. We need to be front footed to respond to both. First, the market... Second, AI is changing how we work. Over the past year, I've watched engineers use AI to ship in days what used to take a team weeks.

source.title: Crypto exchange Coinbase to cut about 14% of workforce — Reuters (May 5, 2026)
source.excerpt: Coinbase said on Tuesday it will cut about 700 jobs, or about 14% of its global workforce, as part of a restructuring plan aimed at reducing costs and repositioning the business for the AI era. Coinbase expects to incur about $50 million to $60 million in total restructuring expenses, mainly tied to employee severance and other termination benefits.

payload.value: Reuters reports Coinbase shares up 4.66% in premarket trading on the announcement. Bitcoin Magazine notes this is the third major round (after 18% in June 2022 and 20% in January 2023), but is the first framed primarily as an AI-driven structural redesign rather than a downturn response.
